last_digest.title: North Korean hackers targeted ethnic Koreans in China with Android ‘BirdCall’ malware
last_digest.rss_summary: Researchers at cybersecurity firm ESET attributed the campaign to APT37 and said the hackers used a backdoor attached to a suite of card games from a company called Sqgame.

last_digest.title: ScarCruft hackers push BirdCall Android malware via game platform
last_digest.rss_summary: The North Korean hacker group APT37 has been delivering an Android version of a backdoor called BirdCall in a supply-chain attack through a video game platform. [...]

last_digest.title: Vimeo confirms breach via third-party vendor impacts 119K users
last_digest.rss_summary: Hackers stole data of 119,000 Vimeo users in April. The breach, linked to a third‑party vendor, exposed personal details. Vimeo confirmed a data breach after the ShinyHunters gang stole personal information of 119,000 users in April 2026. According to Have I Been Pwned, the attackers accessed user data through a compromise at Anodot, a third‑party […]

last_digest.title: Karakurt extortion gang ‘cold case’ negotiator gets 8.5 years in prison
last_digest.rss_summary: A Latvian national extradited to the United States was sentenced to 8.5 years in prison for his "cold case" negotiator role in the Russian Karakurt ransomware group. [...]

last_digest.title: Government, Scientific Entities Hit via Daemon Tools Supply Chain Attack
last_digest.rss_summary:
While trojanized Daemon Tools versions were installed worldwide, a sophisticated backdoor was dropped only on a dozen systems.
The post Government, Scientific Entities Hit via Daemon Tools Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

last_week_digest.title: Medtronic discloses security incident after ShinyHunters claimed theft of 9M+ records
last_week_digest.tldr: Medical device giant Medtronic confirmed a significant security breach after the threat actor ShinyHunters claimed to have stolen over 9 million records from its corporate IT systems. While the company maintains that patient safety and manufacturing operations remain unaffected, the incident highlights the persistent vulnerability of global healthcare infrastructure to large-scale data exfiltration, signalling an urgent need for organisations to further isolate critical networks from broader corporate environments.

last_week_digest.title: North Korean Hackers Target Crypto Firms with ClickFix and AI-Made Zoom Lures
last_week_digest.tldr: North Korean hacking group BlueNoroff has compromised over 100 cryptocurrency organisations across 20 countries using sophisticated AI-driven social engineering. By deploying fake Zoom interfaces and clipboard injection attacks, the threat actors maintained system access for 66 days, specifically targeting executive credentials and digital wallets. This campaign highlights a dangerous shift toward automated deepfake pipelines, signalling that fintech firms must urgently enhance verification protocols against increasingly realistic impersonation tactics.

last_week_digest.title: Home security giant ADT data breach affects 5.5 million people
last_week_digest.tldr: The ShinyHunters extortion group recently breached ADT, compromising the personal data of 5.5 million individuals after successfully executing a vishing attack against an employee’s single sign-on account. This incident, which exposed names, addresses, and partial government identifiers, highlights the severe operational risks posed by targeted social engineering against SaaS platforms. It signals a growing industry trend where attackers bypass robust perimeter defences by exploiting human-centric vulnerabilities within corporate ecosystems.

last_week_digest.title: Burglar alarm biz burgled: ADT confirms cyber intrusion after ShinyHunters extortion attempt
last_week_digest.tldr: Security firm ADT confirmed a cyber intrusion after the ShinyHunters gang claimed to have stolen over 10 million records from its cloud-based environments. While ADT downplayed the incident as a limited data breach, independent analysis suggests at least 5.5 million unique email addresses were compromised. This event signals a growing industry trend where attackers exploit SaaS platforms to bypass perimeter defences, severely damaging institutional trust and operational reputation.

last_week_digest.title: Incomplete Windows Patch Opens Door to Zero-Click Attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: Russia-linked threat actor APT28 is actively exploiting an incomplete Windows patch to conduct zero-click attacks against organisations across Ukraine and the European Union. This security failure exposes critical operational infrastructure to remote compromise, highlighting the persistent risk of state-sponsored espionage. The incident signals a growing industry challenge where insufficient remediation of known vulnerabilities allows sophisticated adversaries to bypass existing defences, forcing businesses to re-evaluate their patch management lifecycles.

last_week_digest.title: BlueNoroff Uses Fake Zoom Calls to Turn Victims Into Attack Lures
last_week_digest.tldr: The North Korean group BlueNoroff is targeting cryptocurrency executives using AI-generated avatars and stolen video footage within fake Zoom meetings. By compromising at least 100 individuals, including numerous CEOs, the threat actors facilitate malware installation through deceptive software update prompts. This sophisticated campaign signals a shift toward weaponising trusted business communication tools, forcing organisations to reconsider the inherent security risks posed by routine virtual professional interactions globally.

last_week_digest.title: ShinyHunters exploit Anodot incident to target Vimeo
last_week_digest.tldr: Vimeo recently confirmed a security breach originating from third-party analytics provider Anodot, exposing metadata, video titles, and customer email addresses. The extortion group ShinyHunters claimed responsibility, threatening to leak stolen data from Snowflake and BigQuery environments unless a ransom is paid. This incident highlights the escalating systemic risk posed by third-party vendor vulnerabilities, signalling that supply chain security remains a critical, unresolved challenge for global digital enterprises.

last_week_digest.title: Signal Phishing Campaign Targets German Officials in Suspected Russian Operation
last_week_digest.tldr: Suspected Russian state-sponsored actors have launched a sophisticated phishing campaign targeting hundreds of German officials, including ministers, military personnel, and journalists, via the Signal messaging platform. By exploiting human trust rather than software vulnerabilities, attackers compromised private communications and sensitive political data. This operation signals a critical shift in global hybrid warfare, where threat actors bypass encryption by weaponising social engineering, highlighting the urgent need for enhanced human-centric security.

last_week_digest.title: China-linked hackers led phishing campaigns targeting journalists and activists, researchers say
last_week_digest.tldr: China-linked hackers utilised over 100 malicious domains to launch two sophisticated phishing campaigns, targeting journalists and activists across a nine-month period. By commissioning freelance contractors, state actors achieved low-cost digital repression while maintaining plausible deniability. This industrialized model signals a dangerous shift in global cyber threats, where offensive capabilities are increasingly commoditised, forcing organisations to recognise that human-centric social engineering remains a primary vector for compromising sensitive international networks.

last_week_digest.title: Critical GitHub Vulnerability Exposed Millions of Repositories
last_week_digest.tldr: A critical remote code execution vulnerability recently impacted GitHub.com and GitHub Enterprise Server, potentially exposing millions of repositories to unauthorised access. This flaw poses significant operational risks, threatening the integrity of proprietary source code and intellectual property for global organisations. The incident signals a growing necessity for robust supply chain security, highlighting how centralised development platforms remain high-value targets that require rigorous, proactive patching to maintain enterprise trust.

last_week_digest.title: Vect 2.0 Ransomware Acts as Wiper, Thanks to Design Error
last_week_digest.tldr: The Vect 2.0 ransomware-as-a-service operation is inadvertently acting as a destructive wiper, permanently destroying files larger than 128KB across Windows, Linux, and VMware ESXi systems. Because the malware discards essential decryption nonces during encryption, victims cannot recover critical databases or backups even if they pay the ransom. This technical failure signals a growing trend of amateurish, flawed malware development that increases operational risk for global enterprises.

last_week_digest.title: CISA orders feds to patch Windows flaw exploited as zero-day
last_week_digest.tldr: CISA has mandated that federal agencies patch the CVE-2026-32202 Windows vulnerability by May 12 following active exploitation. This zero-click flaw, which facilitates NTLM hash theft and lateral network movement, stems from an incomplete previous security update. The incident highlights a growing industry trend where threat actors, including state-sponsored groups like APT28, exploit residual vulnerabilities to compromise sensitive data, forcing organisations to prioritise rapid remediation of recurring software weaknesses.

last_week_digest.title: Microsoft's patch for a 0-day exploited by Russian spies fell short. Another Windows flaw is under attack
last_week_digest.tldr: Microsoft has confirmed active exploitation of CVE-2026-32202, a zero-click Windows authentication coercion flaw resulting from an incomplete patch for a previous vulnerability linked to Russian state-sponsored actors. This security oversight allows attackers to harvest sensitive Net-NTLMv2 credentials, facilitating unauthorised network access and data theft. The incident highlights critical risks in patch efficacy, signalling that incomplete remediation efforts now represent a significant vector for sophisticated persistent threats globally.

last_week_digest.title: Large-scale Roblox hacking operation shut down by Ukrainian authorities
last_week_digest.tldr: Ukrainian authorities have dismantled a sophisticated criminal operation that compromised 610,000 Roblox accounts, generating approximately $225,000 in illicit profits through the sale of high-value digital assets. By exploiting session cookies, the suspects bypassed traditional security measures, highlighting significant vulnerabilities in user authentication protocols. This incident signals a growing trend where cybercriminals increasingly target virtual economies, forcing platforms to bolster account security to protect their global user base.

last_week_digest.title: Checkmarx Confirms Data Stolen in Supply Chain Attack
last_week_digest.tldr: Checkmarx confirmed that attackers exfiltrated sensitive data from its GitHub environment on 30 March, following the injection of malicious code into its supply chain one week prior. This breach exposes the fragility of software development ecosystems, threatening downstream clients with potential supply chain compromises. It signals a critical industry shift where adversaries increasingly target trusted development platforms, forcing organisations to implement more rigorous integrity checks for third-party software.

last_week_digest.title: Three Arrested for Hacking Over 610,000 Roblox Accounts
last_week_digest.tldr: Ukrainian authorities have arrested three individuals for orchestrating a phishing and malware campaign that compromised over 610,000 Roblox accounts. By deploying infostealers, the group identified 357 high-value accounts, generating approximately $225,000 through illicit sales on Russian platforms. This incident highlights the growing financial risks associated with in-game assets and underscores how cybercriminals increasingly exploit social engineering to target digital ecosystems, necessitating robust security measures for virtual economies.

last_week_digest.title: Former incident responders sentenced to 4 years in prison for committing ransomware attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: Two former cybersecurity professionals received four-year prison sentences for orchestrating ransomware attacks using the BlackCat variant. By exploiting their insider knowledge, they targeted medical and engineering firms, successfully extorting $1.3 million from one victim. This case exposes a critical vulnerability within the incident response sector, highlighting how trusted negotiators can weaponise sensitive data. It signals an urgent need for stricter oversight and vetting within global cybersecurity firms.

last_week_digest.title: US ransomware negotiators get 4 years in prison over BlackCat attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: Two former incident response professionals received four-year prison sentences for acting as BlackCat ransomware affiliates, extorting multiple organisations for millions. One victim alone paid $1.27 million following a $10 million demand. This case highlights a critical industry vulnerability: the exploitation of insider access and specialised cybersecurity knowledge by trusted personnel. It signals a growing need for rigorous vetting and internal oversight to mitigate the rising threat of insider-led cybercrime.

last_week_digest.title: France investigates 15-year-old over alleged hack of national ID agency
last_week_digest.tldr: French authorities are investigating a teenager for allegedly breaching the National Agency for Secure Documents, potentially exposing up to 18 million citizens' personal records. This intrusion into critical state infrastructure threatens the integrity of identity management systems and risks widespread identity fraud. The incident highlights a growing trend of youthful threat actors targeting public institutions, signalling an urgent need for governments to fortify digital defences against sophisticated, persistent adversaries.

last_week_digest.title: 1,800 Hit in Mini Shai-Hulud Attack on SAP, Lightning, Intercom
last_week_digest.tldr: The Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack has compromised SAP, Lightning, and Intercom software packages, impacting approximately 1,800 users. With the affected packages recording nearly 10 million monthly downloads, this incident highlights the significant operational risks posed by malicious code injection within widely used open-source dependencies. This event signals a growing industry trend where attackers increasingly target software ecosystems to achieve widespread disruption across global business digital infrastructures.

last_week_digest.title: Instructure confirms data breach, ShinyHunters claims attack
last_week_digest.tldr: Educational technology giant Instructure has confirmed a significant data breach involving the exposure of user names, email addresses, and private messages. While the company investigates, the ShinyHunters extortion group claims to have stolen records for 275 million individuals across 9,000 global institutions. This incident highlights the massive operational risks facing cloud-based service providers, signalling that centralised educational platforms are now primary targets for large-scale, cross-border data extortion campaigns.

last_week_digest.title: 15-year-old detained over massive data breach at French government agency
last_week_digest.tldr: French authorities have detained a 15-year-old suspect following a significant data breach at France Titres, which compromised between 12 and 18 million official records. The incident exposed sensitive personal identifiers, including ID numbers and contact details, forcing a major operational investigation. This event underscores the persistent vulnerability of state-run infrastructure to youthful actors and highlights the critical need for global agencies to fortify digital identity systems against unauthorised access.

last_week_digest.title: Two US cybersecurity experts sentenced in ransomware case, third awaits July ruling
last_week_digest.tldr: Two cybersecurity professionals received four-year prison sentences for deploying ALPHV BlackCat ransomware against five companies between 2023 and 2025. By leveraging their insider expertise, the conspirators extorted $1.2 million from a single victim. This case underscores a critical industry vulnerability: the rise of malicious insiders within security firms. It signals a shift toward stricter vetting of security personnel, as trusted experts increasingly exploit their access for criminal gain.

last_week_digest.title: First reports come in of victims of critical cPanel vuln as 'millions' of sites potentially exposed
last_week_digest.tldr: Attackers are actively exploiting a critical cPanel vulnerability, potentially exposing 1.5 million internet-facing instances to total server compromise. With reports of ransomware demands reaching $7,000, the breach highlights severe operational risks for small businesses relying on managed hosting. This incident signals a growing industry challenge where widespread infrastructure components face rapid weaponisation, forcing providers to balance immediate service availability against the urgent necessity of global security patching.

last_week_digest.title: Google Adjusts Bug Bounties: Chrome Payouts Drop as Android Rewards Rise Amid AI Surge
last_week_digest.tldr: Google has strategically recalibrated its bug bounty programme, elevating the maximum reward for a zero-click Pixel Titan M exploit to $1.5 million while simultaneously reducing payouts for Chrome vulnerabilities. This shift reflects a prioritisation of mobile hardware security over browser-based threats. For global enterprises, this realignment signals that mobile ecosystems are now the primary battleground for sophisticated exploits, necessitating increased investment in securing mobile infrastructure against high-impact attacks.

last_week_digest.title: Silver Fox Springs Tax-Themed Attacks on Orgs in India, Russia
last_week_digest.tldr: The China-backed threat group Silver Fox has launched over 1,600 tax-themed phishing attacks against industrial, retail, and transport organisations across India and Russia. By exploiting administrative urgency to deploy the new ABCDoor backdoor and ValleyRAT, attackers gain persistent remote access to corporate networks. This campaign highlights the persistent vulnerability of human-centric social engineering, signalling that global enterprises must prioritise advanced email security to counter increasingly sophisticated, region-specific lures.

last_week_digest.title: North Korean hackers trojanize gaming platform to spy on ethnic Koreans in China
last_week_digest.tldr: North Korean state-aligned hackers, ScarCruft, compromised the sqgame.net platform to distribute trojanised software to ethnic Koreans in China since late 2024. By injecting backdoors into Windows and Android gaming clients, attackers successfully exfiltrated sensitive personal data and documents. This sophisticated supply chain operation highlights how threat actors weaponise niche digital ecosystems to conduct targeted espionage, signalling a persistent risk to regional security and user privacy globally.

last_week_digest.title: DigiCert breached via malicious screensaver file
last_week_digest.tldr: DigiCert suffered a significant security breach after attackers used social engineering to compromise support systems, leading to the unauthorized issuance of 60 code signing certificates. The incident, which facilitated the distribution of Zhong Stealer malware, highlights critical vulnerabilities in internal endpoint monitoring and support portal access controls. This event signals a growing industry trend where attackers target trusted certificate authorities to undermine global digital supply chain integrity.

last_week_digest.title: Ransomware group claims breach of pro-Orbán Hungarian media firm
last_week_digest.tldr: The ransomware group World Leaks has compromised Hungarian media firm Mediaworks, exfiltrating 8.5 terabytes of sensitive internal data, including payrolls and confidential communications. This breach exposes significant operational vulnerabilities and potential political controversies regarding the firm’s editorial ties to Moscow. The incident signals a growing trend where extortion gangs weaponise stolen internal documents to exert pressure, forcing organisations to navigate complex legal threats while managing severe reputational damage.

last_week_digest.title: Cisco Moves to Acquire Astrix Security to Tackle Non-Human Identity Risks
last_week_digest.tldr: Cisco has announced its acquisition of Astrix Security to address the escalating risks posed by non-human identities within enterprise environments. As machine-to-machine interactions surge, organisations face significant vulnerabilities from unmanaged service accounts and API keys. This strategic move signals a global industry shift toward prioritising identity-centric security frameworks, reflecting a growing necessity to secure automated workflows and AI integrations against sophisticated threats targeting interconnected digital infrastructure.

last_week_digest.title: North Korean hackers targeted ethnic Koreans in China with Android ‘BirdCall’ malware
last_week_digest.tldr: North Korean state-sponsored group APT37 compromised a popular Android gaming platform, deploying the BirdCall backdoor against ethnic Koreans in China. By delivering seven malicious update versions, attackers gained extensive surveillance capabilities, including recording audio and harvesting private data. This supply-chain incident highlights the persistent threat of state-backed espionage targeting vulnerable populations, signalling that mobile ecosystems remain a significant, high-risk vector for sophisticated actors seeking sensitive intelligence globally.

last_week_digest.title: ScarCruft hackers push BirdCall Android malware via game platform
last_week_digest.tldr: North Korean threat actor APT37, known as ScarCruft, has launched a sophisticated supply-chain attack targeting Android users via a Chinese gaming platform. By trojanising APK files with the BirdCall backdoor, the group exfiltrates sensitive personal data and monitors device activity. This campaign highlights the growing risks of third-party software distribution, signalling a strategic shift where state-sponsored actors increasingly exploit niche digital ecosystems to compromise mobile device security globally.

last_week_digest.title: Vimeo confirms breach via third-party vendor impacts 119K users
last_week_digest.tldr: Vimeo confirmed that a breach at third-party analytics provider Anodot resulted in the exposure of personal data belonging to 119,000 users. The ShinyHunters extortion group leaked a 106GB archive containing email addresses and metadata. This incident highlights the growing risks associated with third-party vendor integrations in global supply chains, signalling that organisations must urgently strengthen their oversight of external service providers to prevent cascading data security failures.

last_week_digest.title: Karakurt extortion gang ‘cold case’ negotiator gets 8.5 years in prison
last_week_digest.tldr: Deniss Zolotarjovs, a key negotiator for the Karakurt extortion gang, received an 8.5-year prison sentence for his role in compromising over 54 companies. His activities, which included extorting sensitive health data, contributed to losses exceeding $69 million. This conviction marks a significant milestone in international law enforcement efforts against ransomware syndicates, signalling that global authorities are increasingly successful in tracking and prosecuting individual operatives within these criminal networks.

last_week_digest.title: Government, Scientific Entities Hit via Daemon Tools Supply Chain Attack
last_week_digest.tldr: A sophisticated supply chain attack targeting Daemon Tools software has compromised government and scientific entities globally. While trojanised versions reached a broad user base, attackers deployed a targeted backdoor onto twelve high-value systems. This incident demonstrates how adversaries leverage trusted software distribution channels to bypass perimeter defences, signalling an urgent need for organisations to enhance supply chain visibility and implement rigorous verification protocols for all third-party software updates.
